other_observers: Mike and Mary Blatchley on 2/2/09; I am the original finder and identifier.
specific_location: McIntosh Lake, in the city of Longmont, CO
description: Large, slaty black mantle and upper wing, head neck and under parts bright white. Flattish head and long heavy bill with bright red gonydeal spot. No noticable streaking on head or neck. Legs grayish. Eye color indiscernable.  Size: Larger than any neaby Herring Gull. Taller and heavier looking than Herring. Top of Ring-billed Gull's head even with top of back on this bird.  Color: Blackish upper parts but not as black as primaries.   Legs appeared gray when standing next to Herring Gull, whose legs were noticably pinkish, and next to Ring-billed Gull with obvious yellow legs.Behaviors: nothing notedCall: not notedPlumage: Adult non-breeding
photographer: Photographer: Mike Blatchley, Mike Blatchley, Mike Blatchley
